skill.name: plotly
skill.description: Interactive visualization library for Python. Use it when you need hover tooltips, zoom/pan, selection, animations, or charts embeddable in web pages (e.g., dashboards, exploratory analysis, presentations).
skill.summary: Create interactive Python visualizations with hover tooltips, zoom/pan, and web-embeddable charts.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 353 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a documentation-only workflow for Plotly data visualization. Weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance findings originate from a quality audit JSON data file (plotly_audit_result_v2.json), not from executable code. Ruby/shell backtick execution findings across reference markdown files are Python code examples in Markdown fenced code blocks, not command execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown code fences misidentified as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged Python code blocks in Markdown reference files as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All hits are fenced Python code examples (```python ... ```) demonstrating Plotly API usage. No shell execution occurs.

low_findings.title: JSON audit data flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged strings in plotly_audit_result_v2.json as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. This file is a skill quality evaluation report (not executable code) and contains no cryptographic operations. The detections are false positives from keyword matching.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detection in evaluation data
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged fields in plotly_audit_result_v2.json as system/network reconnaissance. These are evaluation result entries from a legacy audit process, not actual system probing.

low_findings.title: Plotly API call misidentified as dynamic function constructor
low_findings.description: Line 99 of chart-types.md contains `px.ecdf(df, x='value', color='group')` which is a standard Plotly Express ECDF chart API call. The static analyzer misidentified the function call pattern as a dynamic constructor.

low_findings.title: Data column names misidentified as C2 keywords
low_findings.description: Line 23 of chart-types.md contains `['metric1', 'metric2', 'metric3']` which are example column names in a Plotly line chart demo. The term 'metric' matched C2 keyword patterns but is a legitimate data column name.

low_findings.title: Plotly file write documentation flagged as filesystem risk
low_findings.description: Line 47 of export-interactivity.md contains `with open('chart.png', 'wb') as f:` which documents the standard Python method for saving Plotly chart images. This is expected behavior for a visualization export workflow.

low_findings.title: Source attribution URL flagged as hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: Line 7 of SKILL.md contains a GitHub URL linking to the skill source repository. This is standard source attribution in the skill metadata, not unauthorized network access.

content.user_title: Create Interactive Data Visualizations with Plotly
content.value_statement: Creating effective data visualizations requires choosing the right chart type, configuring interactivity, and producing shareable output. This skill gives you a structured workflow with reference guides for Plotly Express, Graph Objects, layout styling, chart types, and export options.
actual_capabilities: Generate interactive scatter, line, bar, histogram, box, and violin plots using Plotly Express
Build custom multi-trace figures with fine-grained control using Graph Objects API
Create subplot arrangements with mixed chart types for dashboard-style layouts
Style visualizations with templates, annotations, shapes, and axis configuration
Export charts as interactive HTML or static images (PNG, SVG, PDF) via Kaleido
Add interactive features: hover tooltips, range sliders, zoom/pan, and box/lasso selection
limitations: Does not work well for static publication figures - use Matplotlib for scientific paper plots
Requires external packages (kaleido) for static image export
Large datasets may cause performance issues in browser rendering
3D charts have limited styling options compared to 2D counterparts

use_cases.title: Exploratory Data Analysis
use_cases.description: Quickly inspect distributions, relationships, and outliers in tabular data using Plotly Express high-level API with automatic hover tooltips and selection tools.
use_cases.target_user: Data scientists and analysts exploring new datasets

use_cases.title: Dashboard and Web Embedding
use_cases.description: Build interactive chart dashboards with subplots, range sliders, and custom interactivity that can be embedded directly into HTML pages or web applications.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers and dashboard engineers building data products

use_cases.title: Presentation and Stakeholder Review
use_cases.description: Create interactive multi-panel figures with zoom, pan, and toggleable traces for live walkthroughs during stakeholder presentations and data reviews.
use_cases.target_user: Analysts and managers presenting data insights to teams

prompt_templates.title: Quick scatter plot from DataFrame
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scatter plot using Plotly Express with the dataframe I will provide. Color the points by a categorical column and add a trendline. Customize the hover template to show precise values.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a pandas DataFrame and needs a quick visualized relationship between two numeric variables

prompt_templates.title: Multi-panel dashboard figure
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Plotly figure with two subplots side by side. The left subplot should be a line chart showing a time series. The right subplot should be a bar chart of aggregated values. Add a shared title and range slider for the time axis.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a compact dashboard view combining time-series and summary data

prompt_templates.title: Custom styled bar chart with annotations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Plotly bar chart using the graph objects API with custom colors, data labels on each bar, and a horizontal reference line at the average value. Use the plotly_white template.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a polished bar chart with annotations for a presentation or report

prompt_templates.title: Export interactive chart to HTML
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an interactive scatter plot with Plotly Express from sample data. Customize the marker size and color scale. Export the figure as a self-contained HTML file that preserves all interactive features.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to share an interactive chart with colleagues who do not have Python installed

output_examples.input: Create a scatter plot showing the relationship between advertising spend and sales revenue.
output_examples.output: An interactive Plotly scatter chart showing each data point with hover tooltips displaying exact spend and revenue values. Points are colored by region with a trendline overlaid. The chart includes zoom, pan, and legend toggle controls.

output_examples.input: Build a dashboard with a line chart of monthly sales and a bar chart of quarterly totals.
output_examples.output: A two-panel dashboard figure. The top panel contains an interactive line chart of monthly sales values with a range slider. The bottom panel shows quarterly totals as a bar chart. Both panels share a common color scheme and title.

output_examples.input: Create an interactive HTML export of a histogram showing age distribution.
output_examples.output: A self-contained HTML file containing an interactive histogram with customizable bin size, hover tooltips showing count and percentage, and box/lasso selection tools for exploring the age distribution data.

best_practices: Use Plotly Express for rapid prototyping and switch to Graph Objects only when you need fine-grained control over individual traces or axes.
Always set a template (plotly_white or plotly_dark) early in figure construction to ensure consistent styling across all traces and subplots.
Use write_html with include_plotlyjs='cdn' for smaller file sizes and fall back to 'directory' for offline viewing.
anti_patterns: Do not use Plotly for high-volume static figure generation where every figure is unique. Use Matplotlib or Seaborn for batch publication-quality static plots.
Do not pass entire large DataFrames with hundreds of thousands of rows directly into Plotly Express without aggregation. Downsample or aggregate data first to ensure browser performance.
Do not ignore the hovertemplate customization. Default tooltips can show raw internal data formats. Always format hover text for the target audience.

faq.question: What is the difference between Plotly Express and Graph Objects?
faq.answer: Plotly Express is a high-level API that creates common chart types from DataFrames with minimal code. Graph Objects is a low-level API for building custom figures with complete control over every element. Plotly Express returns a Graph Objects Figure, so you can use both in the same workflow.

faq.question: How do I export a Plotly chart as a static image?
faq.answer: Use fig.write_image('filename.png'). This requires the Kaleido package to be installed. Supported formats include PNG, SVG, PDF, and JPEG. For interactive export, use fig.write_html('filename.html').

faq.question: Can I combine Plotly Express and Graph Objects in one figure?
faq.answer: Yes. Every Plotly Express function returns a go.Figure object. You can call update_layout, add_trace, add_hline, and all other Graph Objects methods on the result.

faq.question: How do I add a range slider to my time series chart?
faq.answer: Call fig.update_xaxes(rangeslider_visible=True) on your figure. This adds an interactive range slider below the main plot for navigating dense time-series data.

faq.question: Does Plotly support 3D charts?
faq.answer: Yes. Plotly supports 3D scatter, surface, mesh, and line plots through the Graph Objects API (go.Scatter3d, go.Surface, etc.). These support rotation, zoom, and hover interactivity.

faq.question: How can I reduce the file size of interactive HTML exports?
faq.answer: Use include_plotlyjs='cdn' in write_html to load Plotly JS from a CDN instead of embedding it. For very large datasets, consider data aggregation or using webgl renderers.
